title: cirsium × woodwardii
intro: Cirsium × woodwardii, also known as the Wood's thistle, is a hybrid plant species within the thistle genus, resulting from the cross between Cirsium vulgare and Cirsium dissectum. The medicinal properties of Cirsium × woodwardii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is known for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Cirsium × woodwardii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, often in the form of capsules, tablets, or dried flowers for infusion in hot water.
